questions.text: The gang is bored on the couch at Central Perk and someone asks what you should all do tonight.

options.label: You rattle off half a dozen wild plans before the coffee even arrives
You toss out a couple of fun ideas worth trying
You have one idea, and you share it
You wait to hear what everyone else wants to do
You have nothing; you would rather just go along with the plan

questions.text: You walk into an empty apartment that just came up for rent and are asked what could be done with it.

options.label: You picture the whole place transformed, down to the throw pillows
You imagine it clearly enough to describe it
You see roughly what it might become
You struggle to picture much beyond the bare walls
You see only an empty room; imagining more is a waste of time

questions.text: Someone is explaining the absurdly tangled web of who is dating, dumping, and avoiding whom.

options.label: You have the whole mess figured out before they finish
You follow it easily
You get there after a moment's thought
You need it explained a second time
You give up; that kind of thing just loses you

questions.text: Late at night the conversation drifts into big questions about life, fate, and the meaning of it all.

options.label: That kind of talk bores you; you would rather order pizza
You have little patience for it, but you sit through it
You will join in if it turns to something practical
You find the ideas worth chewing on
You love a good deep conversation for its own sake

questions.text: A friend is stuck and needs a clever, made-up excuse to get out of an awkward date.

options.label: Nothing comes to you; making things up just is not in you
You manage little; that kind of inventiveness escapes you
You can force out one plain, obvious excuse
A decent cover story takes shape after some thought
Three ridiculous excuses spring to mind at once

questions.text: You are hosting Thanksgiving dinner for the whole group this year.

options.label: Every dish timed to the minute, a backup plan for each one
A solid menu planned and the schedule set
A rough plan, good enough to start cooking
You will figure it out once people show up
You had not really thought about it

questions.text: You are signing a new apartment lease the landlord just slid across the table.

options.label: You catch every clause and the fine print buried at the bottom
You read it closely and flag the key terms
You skim it for the main points
You trust that it is probably fine
You just sign where they point

questions.text: You share an apartment, and the state of the place is largely up to you.

options.label: Everything labeled and in its place, with a secret closet kept just so
You keep it mostly tidy and the chores done
Neat where it matters, messy everywhere else
You let it slide and deal with the mess later
Chaos suits you fine; cleaning up is overrated

questions.text: You volunteer to handle the group's money for a weekend trip away.

options.label: Receipts lost and the math all wrong; it is a disaster by Sunday
Things slip and get muddled more often than not
A few mistakes creep in, nothing serious
It mostly stays in order
You keep it flawless, every dollar accounted for

questions.text: It is your turn to do the dull errand nobody wants, like the laundry or the grocery run.

options.label: You talk someone else into doing it; it can wait
You put it off whenever you can
You do it, grumbling and late
You get it done without complaint
Your turn is your turn; you would never pass it off

questions.text: It is a packed party and the music is loud and the place is full of people.

options.label: You are the center of it, leading the toasts and the dancing
You are lively and pull people in around you
You enjoy yourself with a few good friends
You hang near the edges of the room
You slip out early; crowds like this wear you out

questions.text: You walk into a party where you don't know a single person in the room.

options.label: You are introducing yourself before your coat is even off
You happily strike up talk with whoever is nearby
You will say something if there is a good reason
You wait for someone to come to you
You say nothing unless you absolutely have to

questions.text: The room quiets and everyone turns to hear the big story you are about to tell.

options.label: You love every eye on you; the floor is yours
You are comfortable holding the room
You manage it well enough
You would rather the attention move on quickly
You freeze up and wish someone else would talk

questions.text: The whole group is hanging out, talking over each other in the crowded apartment.

options.label: You sit back and watch, taking it all in, saying little
You hang back more often than not
You jump in only when you have something to add
You make yourself heard in the mix
You put yourself right at the center of it all

questions.text: You are seated next to a friend's date you have never met before.

options.label: You stay quiet and let the silence sit
You speak only when spoken to
You offer a few careful words
You chat with them easily enough
You are talking like old friends within minutes

questions.text: A friend breaks down on the couch, devastated by a brutal breakup.

options.label: You feel their heartbreak as your own and sit with them for hours
You are moved, and you offer real comfort
You acknowledge it and try to move them along
You notice, but feel little yourself
You tell them to get over it already

questions.text: A friend begs for your help moving apartments on the one free day you had all month.

options.label: You drop your plans entirely and show up to help all day
You make the time to lend a hand
You swing by for a little while
You suggest they ask someone else
You wave it off; you had plans of your own

questions.text: Someone new and nervous joins the group and sits stiffly, not saying a word.

options.label: You go out of your way to make them feel welcome
You are warm and friendly toward them
You are polite enough
You ignore how awkward they feel
You let them squirm

questions.text: Someone takes a sly little dig at you in front of the whole group, fishing for a reaction.

options.label: You fire back with the most cutting line you have
You return the insult sharply
You give a measured comeback
You let it slide without a word
You answer the dig with plain kindness

questions.text: You are at a gathering full of new faces and unfamiliar names.

options.label: Other people's lives do not interest you in the least
You have little curiosity about any of them
You will listen if it happens to involve you
You are fairly curious about the people here
You want to know everyone's whole story before the night is out

questions.text: Two crises hit at once: your date is at the door and dinner is burning on the stove.

options.label: Your nerves fray on the spot; the pressure undoes you
You feel the strain of it hard
You are rattled, but still moving
You stay fairly steady through it
Pressure like this barely touches you

questions.text: It is the night before a big day whose outcome you cannot be sure of.

options.label: You lie awake running through every way it could go wrong
You worry over it well into the night
It crosses your mind, then you fall asleep
You give it little thought
You sleep just fine; worrying changes nothing

questions.text: One of those days arrives with a small win and a small disaster around every corner.

options.label: Your mood swings from giddy to crushed and back again
Your mood shifts often through the day
You have your ups and downs
You stay mostly even
Your mood holds steady from morning to night

questions.text: You go about the ordinary hassles of life, the daily pile of little demands.

options.label: You are at ease most days, whatever comes
You are generally relaxed
Calm on some days, tense on others
You are often on edge
You are rarely relaxed at all

questions.text: A long, gray stretch settles in, the kind of slump where nothing much goes right.

options.label: Low spirits seldom find you; you keep your humor
You rarely sink into the gloom
You have your darker stretches now and then
The blues visit you often
The gloom rarely lifts from you at all
